features.name: Live streams
features.description: Push and pull live streams with stream keys and ingest configuration.

features.name: Pull streams
features.description: Stream inputs pulled from upstream sources (RTSP, SRT, RIST, HLS, etc.) instead of pushed, with resolution-stage lifecycle audit.
features.gap_reason: Resolution-stage lifecycle events ship: Foghorn writes pull_source_events rows for STREAM_SOURCE evaluations (resolved / not_found / disabled / blocked_uri / private_not_allowed / commodore_error / foghorn_base_unresolved), exposed via Stream.recentPullSourceEvents and shown on the stream setup panel. Downstream MistServer dial telemetry needs new upstream connect/disconnect/retry trigger types before FrameWorks can show dial-stage health.

features.name: Stream keys
features.description: Per-stream ingest credentials with rotation.

features.name: Playback
features.description: Resolve a viewer or ingest endpoint for a stream / VOD / clip.

features.name: Playback access control
features.description: Public / JWT / webhook playback authorization policy on streams, clips, and VOD assets, with a dry-run tester and full agent management surface.

examples.title: Test a JWT against the live policy
Inspect webhook policy without firing
Fire a real webhook test

features.name: Signing keys
features.description: ES256 keypairs for signing playback JWTs. Private material returned once on creation.

features.name: Multistream push targets
features.description: Forward live streams to additional RTMP/SRT destinations.

features.name: DVR recordings
features.description: 24/7-capable DVR recording with bounded live seekback, continuous archive storage, chapter replay, and VOD-style playback.

features.name: DVR chapters
features.description: Chapter artifacts that segment a long-running DVR session into replayable windows, so a 24/7 recording can be replayed by window, civil-time range, or explicit range without restarting the live stream.
features.gap_reason: Chapter playback and policy controls are present in the dashboard; audit-event coverage for chapter-policy changes still needs verification.

features.name: Clips
features.description: Time-range clips extracted from streams or recordings.

features.name: VOD assets
features.description: Multipart VOD upload and lifecycle.

features.name: Media retention
features.description: Tenant-controlled storage policy for 24/7 DVR archives, clips, and VOD assets, with per-asset overrides and entitlement bounds.

examples.title: Read per-class defaults + tier cap + effective horizons
Keep VOD forever (paid tier)
Set clip default to 14 days
Per-stream override (DVR 90d, clip cleared)
Per-asset override (clip kept forever)

features.name: Thumbnails and sprite previews
features.description: Auto-generated poster frames and sprite-tile preview strips for live streams, clips, VOD, and DVR playback.
features.gap_reason: Apps can read Chandler poster, sprite JPG, and sprite VTT URLs through resolveViewerEndpoint metadata and the resolve_playback_endpoint MCP tool. The webapp builds bulk library thumbnails by combining streamingConfig.chandlerDomain with stream IDs and artifact hashes from list queries. The remaining gap is a first-class bulk asset URL surface for external apps and agents: streams/clips/DVR/VOD list queries should return ready-to-use preview asset URLs or a shared helper contract, without requiring one resolveViewerEndpoint call per asset. Generation status, regeneration, and troubleshooting remain follow-on operational controls.

examples.title: Fetch poster and sprite URLs
Fetch the Chandler host for a library grid

features.name: Gateway-backed transcoding
features.description: Live ABR renditions through Livepeer Gateway or local processing, with processing usage telemetry.
features.gap_reason:
The media plane can use Livepeer Gateway-backed processing and the analytics API exposes processing/orchestrator telemetry.
What is still missing is a tenant-facing control surface for rendition ladders, plan-aware defaults, per-stream overrides,
and agent/dashboard workflows for choosing when Gateway-backed ABR is enabled.

features.name: Analytics
features.description: Stream health, viewers, geographic, routing, quality tiers, federation, node metrics, and expanding QoE diagnostics.
features.gap_reason:
Stream, routing, server-side QoS, and current client QoE summaries are live across API, dashboard, MCP, and docs.
The QoE Analytics RFC is still active: public SDK-to-platform telemetry ingest, TTFF/rebuffering/failure/quality-change capture,
non-npm player support, sampling/retention policy, and view-level dashboards are not fully productized yet.

features.name: Cluster marketplace
features.description: Browse listed clusters and manage subscriptions. A vetted public marketplace program for third-party capacity is on the roadmap.
features.gap_reason: Marketplace primitives ship across the API, MCP, dashboard, and docs: listing, preferred cluster selection, invites, subscription requests, approval, pricing models, monthly checkout, and metered/custom rating paths. What is still missing is the full public marketplace operating model: operator vetting, published commercial terms, support boundaries, and production onboarding workflow for third-party capacity.

features.name: Edge clusters
features.description: Tenant-owned edge clusters with bootstrap and node enrollment.

features.name: Enrollment / bootstrap tokens
features.description: One-shot tokens used to enroll new edge nodes into a cluster.

features.name: Cluster invites
features.description: Direct invites granting another tenant access to a cluster.

features.name: Cluster subscription requests
features.description: Tenant-requested subscription to a cluster, requiring operator approval.

features.name: Nodes (edge / ingest / playback)
features.description: Per-node health, info, and discovery.

features.name: Node mode (drain / maintenance)
features.description: Operator controls to drain a node, put it in maintenance, or restore it to active service.

examples.title: Drain a node
Restore a node

features.name: Federated network status
features.description: Cross-cluster routing visibility and orchestrator vantage points.

mcp.reason: Diagnostic surface; covered by analytics MCP tools.

features.name: Managed DNS and cluster domains
features.description: Platform-managed DNS, TLS certificates, media-cluster domains, and tenant streaming domain discovery.
features.gap_reason:
Navigator reconciles platform and media-cluster DNS for operated deployments, and the API exposes tenant streaming domains
through streamingConfig. The missing product surface is customer-controlled DNS: custom domain onboarding, verification,
certificate status, proxy policy, and self-serve dashboard/MCP workflows.

features.name: Billing & prepaid balance
features.description: Card / Mollie / crypto top-ups, billing details, invoices, prepaid balance.

features.name: Wallet & on-chain identity
features.description: Wallet sign-in, linked-wallet account identity, and x402 micropayments.
features.gap_reason:
Agent wallet auth and x402 settlement are exposed through get_payment_options and submit_payment.
First-class MCP wallet-management tools for listing, linking, and unlinking additional wallets are still missing.

features.name: Developer API tokens
features.description: Long-lived API tokens for SDK / CI use.

mcp.reason: API tokens authorize MCP itself; tools managing them would be circular.

features.name: GraphQL playground
features.description: Interactive GraphQL explorer wired to the live schema.

graphql.reason: The playground IS a graphql client; nothing to expose.

mcp.reason: Agents use introspect_schema / generate_query / execute_query directly.

features.name: Agents (MCP server)
features.description: Model Context Protocol server exposing FrameWorks operations to agents.

graphql.reason: MCP exposes GraphQL via execute_query; nothing additional to mutate.

features.name: Skipper (in-app consultant agent)
features.description: Conversational platform agent for support and investigations.
features.gap_reason: MCP exposes ask_consultant only; conversation management (list/get/send/archive) is not exposed.

features.name: Tenant settings
features.description: Tenant identity, billing setup, retention defaults, routing preferences, and wallet-provisioned account lifecycle.
features.gap_reason:
Core tenant account setup is live: wallet-provisioned accounts, tenant name, billing details, billing tier/balance, storage retention defaults,
and preferred cluster/routing settings are exposed. Broader tenant administration is still expanding: team/RBAC, notification preferences,
custom domains/branding, security controls, and account lifecycle controls are not yet complete across API, dashboard, MCP, and docs.

features.name: Outbound event webhooks
features.description: Tenant-configured outbound webhooks for stream / clip / VOD lifecycle events.
features.gap_reason: Not shipped. Inbound playback-auth webhook exists (covered by playback-access-control); outbound delivery is future work.

features.name: Teams & RBAC
features.description: Multi-user tenants with role-based access control.
features.gap_reason: Not shipped. Requires tenant membership model, role grants, invitation flow, GraphQL policy checks, MCP permission scoping, and webapp team administration.

features.name: Processing and composition
features.description: Server-side processing in the media engine — MistServer-native blitter for overlays, logos, and lower-thirds; Composer for server-side ad insertion (SSAI) and raw-pixel composition; transcription, object detection, picture-in-picture, and automated clipping on top.
features.gap_reason: Not shipped. Requires product/API design for overlay placement and lifecycle, SSAI ad-decision integration, model execution boundaries for AI detection and transcription, billing meters, agent permissions, and dashboard/MCP surfaces for configuring composition.

features.name: Usage caps, alerts, retention cost preview
features.description: Customer-set caps and alerts on storage / bandwidth / recording / transcode meters; cost-preview hypotheticals for retention policy changes.
features.gap_reason: Cost preview shipped (2026-05-16): Purser surfaces per-tenant marginal storage pricing via GetTenantBillingStatus.StoragePricing, and pkg/billing/storagecost projects a per-asset $/day + $/month figure used on Clip / DVRRequest / VodAsset.storageCost. The storage browser route lists assets with their cost projection and resolved retention. Remaining gap: customer-set caps (configurable thresholds per meter) and alert delivery infrastructure (dedupe, notification channels, threshold-crossed semantics) — still real engineering projects, tracked as the rest of this row.

features.name: Scheduled recordings
features.description: Calendar-driven DVR captures that start and stop on schedule, so streams record themselves on a fixed cadence.
features.gap_reason: DVR recording is shipped (start_dvr / stop_dvr exist); scheduling is the gap. Requires a schedule store, a sweeper that triggers DVR start/stop at window boundaries, GraphQL mutation + query surface, MCP tools, and a calendar UI. Primary use cases include places of worship, lectures, periodic events, and any scheduled programming.

features.name: Scheduled VOD streams
features.description: Linear live channels assembled from existing VOD assets, with schedules that make archived media play as a rolling live stream.
features.gap_reason:
Not shipped. Managed Mist-native streams provide the lower-level primitive for platform-owned concrete stream configs, but scheduled VOD streams need a separate product layer:
a schedule/timeline store, VOD artifact selection, asset availability checks, pre-hydration/pinning for frozen assets, failover-aware edge placement, GraphQL/MCP controls,
and dashboard scheduling workflows. The source material should remain normal finalized VOD artifacts with their existing storage lifecycle; the scheduler turns them into a
live channel without bypassing artifact ownership, retention, or billing semantics.

features.name: Incidents and alerts
features.description: Tenant-scoped incident feed aggregating node-health, certificate, DNS, capacity, and cluster alerts with acknowledge/resolve and realtime delivery.
features.gap_reason: Not shipped. Prometheus and Grafana cover internal monitoring. The customer-facing gap is a tenant incident feed — Alertmanager webhook ingestion, dedup/fingerprinting, an incident store, GraphQL queries + acknowledge/resolve mutations, realtime delivery via Signalman, and MCP tools so agents can triage. Self-hosted operators get visibility into their own clusters (cert expiry, port reachability, DNS resolution, CPU/memory thresholds) without standing up a separate monitoring stack.

features.name: DRM
features.description: FairPlay, Widevine, and PlayReady protection for premium playback workflows.
features.gap_reason: Not shipped. Playback signing keys and webhook/JWT access control are available today, but multi-DRM license packaging and player enforcement are separate work.

features.name: Device discovery and contribution inputs
features.description: Edge-local discovery for cameras, PTZ devices, NDI sources, USB capture, HDMI inputs, and local contribution paths.
features.gap_reason:
Experimental media-engine discovery and local-source creation exist below the FrameWorks product layer. The missing work is
packaging that capability into FrameWorks Edge, then exposing it through API, dashboard, Tray, CLI, and MCP workflows for managed
streams and self-hosted clusters.

features.name: Standalone object and block storage
features.description: Customer-facing storage buckets, volumes, policies, and lifecycle controls outside the video-asset workflow.
features.gap_reason:
FrameWorks already has media storage, cold-storage lifecycle, and cross-cluster S3 delegation for DVR, clips, VOD, thumbnails,
and preview assets. A standalone object/block storage product is not shipped: it needs resource provisioning, quota/billing,
access credentials, policy management, dashboard/API/MCP controls, and operator support boundaries.

features.name: Generic proxying and CDN services
features.description: Customer-configured proxy/CDN front doors for non-video services and adjacent workloads.
features.gap_reason:
Navigator can proxy selected platform services for operated deployments. A customer-facing generic proxy/CDN product is not shipped:
it needs origin onboarding, routing policy, TLS/domain validation, caching controls, abuse controls, billing, and dashboard/API/MCP
workflows.

features.name: Mobile apps and integrated help center
features.description: Native mobile operations and an in-product help center for support, incidents, and account workflows.
features.gap_reason: Not shipped as a product surface. Support and messaging infrastructure exists, but native mobile workflows and integrated help-center UX are planned after core platform surfaces.
